loops._spawn_isolation_note: Loop body runs inline in the main agent (Opus) for quality; the Step-3 implementation reviewer and any HALT_SUCCESS challenger are still spawned as independent subagents, preserving both independence-critical gates.
loops.verdict_explanation: The tool works correctly and the code is honest — no fake architecture, no misleading abstractions — but its central data carrier is an anonymous 11-field positional tuple, its search engine is a single ~720-line function that fuses transport, parsing, filtering, date inference and terminal rendering, and there are zero automated tests. Ownership is clean and the program is trivially concurrency-safe (synchronous), so the weaknesses are structural (domain modeling, decomposition, regression resistance), not correctness or safety.

architecture_quality.proof: search_boards fuses transport (soundboard-snag.py:787,855), regex parsing (860-903), filtering (1046-1100), date inference (962-1044) and rendering (1105-1141,1268-1334) in one function

state_management.proof: single-threaded; SoundboardSnag attrs immutable post-__init__ (soundboard-snag.py:290-293); search_boards locals have one writer each; no multi-writer or hidden state machines

domain_modeling.proof: no domain types pre-refactor; central record was an anonymous 11-tuple (soundboard-snag.py:1145 pre-loop), sounds_info is bare 2-tuples, approx_source is stringly-typed

data_flow.proof: positional 11-tuple was an implicit unenforced contract across construction + 3 read sites; main re-implements result filtering (soundboard-snag.py:1525) duplicating search_boards' own filter (1181)

framework_idioms.proof: idiomatic stdlib use (with urlopen, argparse, os.path); _sanitize_filename (soundboard-snag.py:383-441) is genuinely defensive — neutralizes path traversal, reserved names, control chars; HTTPS BASE_URL; no secrets

concurrency.proof: no async/threading/multiprocessing imports; purely synchronous CLI — no shared mutable concurrency, no reentrancy, no task lifetime to manage
concurrency.residual_blocking_10: time.sleep pacing (soundboard-snag.py:595,1156,1176) is the only timing; intentional server etiquette, not a determinism hazard
concurrency.residual_rationale_or_backlog_ref: Tool is deliberately synchronous (zero-dependency stdlib CLI). There is no concurrency model to make safer; the sleeps are network etiquette, not a hazard. Permanent design carve-out.

simplicity.proof: one ~720-line function (soundboard-snag.py:612-1334) ~6 levels deep; date-display render duplicated near-verbatim (1121-1136 vs 1286-1301); dead wrapper _fetch_last_modified (223-226)

test_strategy.proof: zero test files; deterministic network-free helpers untested — _sanitize_filename (383), _parse_views_count (94), _parse_http_datetime (163); a suffix-flip mutation at _parse_views_count:118-121 would not be caught

credibility.proof: code is honest (does what it claims, no fake-clean), but a senior reviewer reading in source order hits the 11-tuple (1145) and 720-line engine (612) and zero tests, undercutting architectural confidence

strengths: Filename sanitization is genuinely defensive, not cosmetic — soundboard-snag.py:383-441 decodes HTML entities, strips UUID patterns, neutralizes path traversal (/ and \ replaced), control chars, and Windows reserved names from untrusted header/title input
Honest synchronous design: zero shared-concurrency hazards because there is no concurrency — REQUEST_DELAY pacing (soundboard-snag.py:595,1156,1176) is deliberate server etiquette
Zero third-party dependencies held to genuinely (stdlib-only imports, soundboard-snag.py:60-72), matching the project's stated constraint

findings.title: search_boards returns an anonymous 11-field positional tuple
findings.why_it_matters: The primary data carrier of the search engine forces every caller to know that index 8 is views_int and index 9 is approx_updated; reordering the tuple breaks all callers silently — a hazard the project's own CLAUDE.md explicitly warns about.
findings.what_is_wrong: search_boards builds and returns 11-element bare tuples that callers read by position (r[1], x[8], x[9], results[0][1]) and by an 11-name destructure repeated in two render loops; the field meaning lives only in a docstring.
findings.leverage_impact: Callers learn far too much (positional layout of 11 fields) for what the interface delivers.
findings.locality_impact: A field reorder or insertion forces coordinated edits at every read site with no compiler or test to catch a miss.
findings.why_weakens_submission: Anonymous positional tuples as the central domain record are the textbook weak-domain-model smell; they make the search engine's output fragile and hard to read.
findings.minimal_correction_path: Introduce a BoardResult typing.NamedTuple (Python 3.6+ compatible; still a tuple at runtime so the change is behavior-preserving) and convert every construction and read site to named-field access.

findings.title: search_boards is a ~720-line function fusing transport, parsing, filtering, date inference and rendering
findings.why_it_matters: The core scraping logic cannot be tested or reused because it is welded to network I/O and terminal printing, so regression resistance and architecture both suffer.
findings.what_is_wrong: A single function (soundboard-snag.py:612-1334) performs HTTP fetching, regex HTML extraction, domain filtering, best-effort date inference, sorting and all terminal rendering, interleaved inside one deeply nested loop.
findings.leverage_impact: Maintainers must hold the entire function in their head to change any one concern.
findings.locality_impact: A parsing change risks the rendering and filtering paths because they share one scope and 20+ locals.
findings.why_weakens_submission: Fusing pure parsing with I/O and presentation is the primary reason the codebase has no unit tests — the testable logic is unreachable without the network.
findings.minimal_correction_path: Extract the pure per-board HTML->BoardResult parsing into a module-level function that takes html text and returns the data, leaving transport and rendering in search_boards; this makes the parse unit-testable without network. Prove friction first (done: no parse test can exist today). Do over multiple loops; do not introduce a class hierarchy.

findings.title: Zero automated tests; deterministic helpers untested; hyphenated module name blocks import
findings.why_it_matters: Multi-branch pure logic (filename sanitization, views parsing, HTTP date parsing) can regress silently with no signal, and the file name itself obstructs even writing a test.
findings.what_is_wrong: No test files exist. Network-free, deterministic functions with non-trivial branching are untested, and the module file is named soundboard-snag.py (a hyphen is not a valid identifier) so it cannot be imported without importlib gymnastics.
findings.leverage_impact: Without tests at the helper interfaces, every refactor of those helpers is unverified.
findings.locality_impact: A sanitization regression would corrupt output filenames across every download with no failing test.
findings.why_weakens_submission: Absence of tests on stateful/parsing logic is the binding regression-resistance gap and caps the test-strategy dimension.
findings.minimal_correction_path: Add test_soundboard_snag.py using stdlib unittest, loading the module via importlib.util.spec_from_file_location; cover _sanitize_filename, _parse_views_count, _parse_http_datetime, _extract_board_slugs_from_search_html with table-driven cases.

findings.title: Dead pass-through wrapper _fetch_last_modified
findings.why_it_matters: Unused indirection adds reading cost and a second name for one behavior with no caller to justify it.
findings.what_is_wrong: _fetch_last_modified wraps _fetch_last_modified_detailed and discards the diagnostic, but nothing in the module calls it; the live path uses _fetch_last_modified_detailed and the fetch_last_modified_cached closure.
findings.leverage_impact: None — the wrapper buys nothing.
findings.locality_impact: Minor: a reader must confirm it is unused before trusting the live path.
findings.why_weakens_submission: A pass-through wrapper with no caller is dead weight that the deletion test removes cleanly.
findings.minimal_correction_path: Delete _fetch_last_modified (soundboard-snag.py:223-226). Confirm debug_track_dates.py does not import it (it cannot — hyphenated module name).

findings.title: Date-display and skipped-breakdown rendering duplicated near-verbatim
findings.why_it_matters: Two copies of the same presentation logic must be kept in sync by hand; they will drift.
findings.what_is_wrong: The approx-updated date block and the skipped-buckets breakdown block each appear twice with near-identical bodies.
findings.leverage_impact: A formatting change must be made in two places.
findings.locality_impact: Drift between the two copies produces inconsistent output for the same data.
findings.why_weakens_submission: Duplicate presentation logic is a simplification deduction and compounds the god-function problem in F2.
findings.minimal_correction_path: Once F2 begins separating rendering from searching, extract the two duplicated blocks into small module-level format helpers that take a BoardResult and the date-stats map; do not extract before the rendering is separated (avoid premature helper).

simplification_check.structurally_necessary: F1 BoardResult resolves the weak-domain-model / fragile-positional-contract finding — Shallow-module test now passes (interface carries named fields, not positions). Behavior-preserving because NamedTuple is still a tuple.
simplification_check.helpful_simplification: F4 (delete dead _fetch_last_modified) and F5 (de-duplicate render blocks) are subtractive follow-ups.
simplification_check.should_not_be_done: Do not split the single file into a package, add a class hierarchy for parsing, or introduce a port/adapter for HTTP — the tool is intentionally a zero-dependency single-file CLI and friction for a network seam is not proven (one production transport, no test fake planned).
simplification_check.tests_after_fix: BoardResult is a domain type; its index+attr compatibility and sort-key usage were smoke-verified this loop. Dedicated helper tests land in F3 (next priority), where test_soundboard_snag.py will assert sanitization/parsing at the helper interfaces.

backlog.title: Replace search_boards' 11-field positional tuple with a BoardResult NamedTuple (F1)
backlog.why_it_matters: Biggest single readability/robustness gain; removes the silent-reorder hazard CLAUDE.md warns about.

backlog.title: Add a stdlib unittest suite for the pure helpers (F3)
backlog.why_it_matters: Test strategy is the binding constraint; sanitization/parsing have branchy logic that can regress silently.

backlog.title: Extract pure per-board HTML parsing out of search_boards (F2)
backlog.why_it_matters: Makes the core scraping logic testable and shrinks the god-function.

deepening_candidates.source_friction_proven: Finding F2 — the parse logic is unreachable for testing because it is fused with network I/O and printing
deepening_candidates.why_shallow_or_misplaced: There is no parsing interface at all; the behavior is inlined in a 720-line function so it has neither Depth nor a Seam.
deepening_candidates.behavior_to_move_behind_interface: Regex extraction of board name, downloads flag, sound ids/titles, description, category, views, tags from one board's HTML, returning a BoardResult (minus the network-derived approx date).
deepening_candidates.test_surface_after_change: test_soundboard_snag.py asserts parsing on saved HTML fixtures without network.
deepening_candidates.smallest_first_step: Extract _parse_board_html(html, board_name) returning the parsed fields; call it from the loop.
deepening_candidates.what_not_to_do: Do not introduce a parser class hierarchy or a strategy pattern; one pure function is the honest shape.

builder_notes.pattern: Anonymous positional tuple used as a multi-field domain record
builder_notes.how_to_recognize: Callers read results via r[8] / x[9] or a long destructure, and the field meaning lives only in a docstring or comment.
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: The moment a tuple has 3+ heterogeneous fields read at more than one site, make it a NamedTuple — it is index-compatible so the change is free.
builder_notes.stack_example: Python: typing.NamedTuple gives attribute access and stays a tuple at runtime, so existing indexing/unpacking keeps working during the migration.

builder_notes.pattern: God-function fusing transport, computation and presentation
builder_notes.how_to_recognize: One function owns 15+ mutable locals, makes network calls, parses data, and prints — and you cannot unit-test any slice of it without the network.
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: Pull the pure computation (parse/filter/format) out as functions that take data and return data; let the I/O function orchestrate.
builder_notes.stack_example: Python: a _parse_board_html(html)->record function is testable against a saved fixture; the network loop just calls it.

builder_notes.pattern: Pass-through wrapper with no caller
builder_notes.how_to_recognize: A thin function delegates to another and drops a return value, but grep finds no call site.
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: Run the deletion test before keeping any wrapper: if removing it makes complexity vanish rather than reappear across callers, delete it.
builder_notes.stack_example: Python: _fetch_last_modified delegating to _fetch_last_modified_detailed with zero callers.

loops.narrative: Place — functionally solid but structurally compromised, and the gap is entirely structural rather than correctness or safety. Ownership is trustworthy (single-threaded, one writer per concern) and concurrency is trustworthy because there is no concurrency. The three deductions that matter are the anonymous 11-tuple domain record (addressed this loop), the 720-line search engine that fuses I/O with logic, and the complete absence of tests. This loop's simplification (named BoardResult) helped and added no ceremony. The dominant risk in future loops is over-reaching toward a package split or HTTP seam the single-file zero-dependency design does not warrant; future work should stay subtractive and add tests, not layers.

loop_result.what_changed: Introduced a BoardResult typing.NamedTuple in soundboard-snag.py and converted search_boards' result construction plus every read site (filter, both sort keys, the results-render loop, the download-suggestion line, and main's search-and-download loop) from positional tuple access to named-field access.
loop_result.evidence_change_is_honest: python3 -m py_compile soundboard-snag.py passes; an importlib smoke test confirms BoardResult is index-compatible (br[1] is br.has_downloads), attribute access works, and the views_int/approx_updated sort keys still order correctly; --help exits 0; grep confirms no positional access of search results remains (L1255 is an unrelated 2-tuple sort, L94 is the new docstring).

implementation_review.reason: All three checks pass: F1's positional access pattern is gone, the BoardResult NamedTuple is behavior-preserving (still a plain tuple at runtime, constructed by keyword so no transposition risk), and no new finding at same-or-higher severity was introduced.

loops._spawn_isolation_note: Loop body inline in main (Opus); Step-3 reviewer + HALT challenger spawned as independent subagents.
loops.verdict_explanation: After loop 1 the central record is a named BoardResult, which lifts domain modeling and data flow. The remaining structural gaps are the ~734-line search_boards function that still fuses transport, parsing, filtering, dates and rendering, and (at the start of this loop) the absence of any tests. This loop adds the missing test surface for the pure helpers.

builder_notes.pattern: Untestable logic because the file name is not importable
builder_notes.how_to_recognize: The module file has a hyphen or other non-identifier char, so `import x` fails and there is no test file at all.
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: Load by path with importlib.util.spec_from_file_location under a clean module name; loading runs only top-level definitions when main() is guarded by __name__ == '__main__'.
builder_notes.stack_example: Python: spec_from_file_location('soundboard_snag', 'soundboard-snag.py') gives an importable handle to a hyphenated file.

builder_notes.pattern: Characterization tests before a risky refactor
builder_notes.how_to_recognize: You are about to extract or move logic that has no tests; a silent behavior change would go unnoticed.
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: Pin current observable behavior of the pure helpers first (including the non-obvious cases), then refactor against a green suite.
builder_notes.stack_example: Python: assert _sanitize_filename('CON.mp3', ...) == '_Con.mp3' captures the exact title-case-then-reserve ordering before you touch it.

builder_notes.pattern: Test the pure surface, not the I/O surface
builder_notes.how_to_recognize: The tempting target is the big network function, but it needs heavy mocking; the real value is in the deterministic helpers it calls.
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: Test the network-free functions directly; only mock I/O after you have extracted the pure logic from it.
builder_notes.stack_example: Python: test _parse_views_count / _parse_http_datetime directly; defer testing search_boards until parsing is extracted.

loops.narrative: Place — still functionally solid and structurally improving. Loop 1's BoardResult lifted domain modeling, data flow, simplicity and credibility with no behavioral change. This loop closes the most glaring regression-resistance gap by adding a 24-case characterization suite over the pure helpers, loaded past the hyphenated-filename obstacle via importlib. Ownership and concurrency remain trustworthy. The next structural move is extracting per-board parsing from the 734-line search engine, now de-risked by the test suite; the standing risk is over-reaching toward a package split or HTTP seam the single-file design does not warrant.

loop_result.what_changed: Added test_soundboard_snag.py, a stdlib unittest suite of 24 characterization tests over the pure network-free helpers (_parse_views_count, _parse_http_datetime, _extract_board_slugs_from_search_html, _quote_path_segment, _extract_filename_from_headers, _sanitize_filename, the date formatters), loading the hyphenated module via importlib.
loop_result.evidence_change_is_honest: python3 -m py_compile passes on both files; python3 -m unittest test_soundboard_snag runs 24 tests, all OK; the assertions pin real current behavior including non-obvious cases (CON.mp3 -> _Con.mp3, audio_42 -> 'Audio 42.mp3', percent-escape preservation, UTC datetime conversion). No production code was touched, so behavior is unchanged by construction.

implementation_review.reason: All three checks pass: F-003 is resolved by a real 186-line test file that loads the hyphenated module via importlib and asserts behavior across all six named pure helpers plus date formatters; every spot-checked assertion reflects actual production code behavior; no production file was modified and the test suite makes no network calls.

What keeps it short of top-tier is the still-large search_boards (transport + filter + date-scan + render remain fused) and two small residuals (a dead wrapper, duplicated render blocks).

builder_notes.pattern: Pure computation fused inside an I/O loop
builder_notes.how_to_recognize: A block of regex/transform code sits between a network read and a print, sharing the loop's locals; you cannot test it without the network.
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: Move the pure block to a module-level function taking the raw input and returning a named record; the loop binds locals from it.
builder_notes.stack_example: Python: _parse_board_html(board_html) -> ParsedBoard makes 10 regex extractions fixture-testable.

builder_notes.pattern: Return a named record from a parser, not a pile of locals
builder_notes.how_to_recognize: The extracted function would otherwise return a long tuple or set many out-params.
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: Give the parser a NamedTuple result so call sites and tests read fields by name.
builder_notes.stack_example: Python: ParsedBoard(NamedTuple) carries sound_matches, has_downloads, download_ids, ... .

builder_notes.pattern: Test the extracted Interface with a synthetic fixture
builder_notes.how_to_recognize: A newly-extracted pure function has no direct test, only transitive coverage through the I/O caller.
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: Write a small representative input string and assert each field of the returned record, including an empty-input case.
builder_notes.stack_example: Python: a 10-line board HTML string asserts download dedup, tag extraction, views parsing, html-unescape of preview titles.

loops.narrative: Place — now a good app, not yet top-tier. The three structural anchors are handled: named BoardResult, a real helper test suite (visible this loop and lifting test_strategy and credibility), and this loop's extraction of the per-board parse into a fixture-tested _parse_board_html. search_boards still owns transport, filtering, the network date-scan and all rendering, so architecture and simplicity have further to climb (scored next loop). Ownership and concurrency remain trustworthy. Remaining backlog is small and subtractive (dead wrapper, duplicated render); the standing risk is over-reaching toward decomposition the single-file design does not need.

loop_result.what_changed: Extracted the pure per-board HTML parsing out of search_boards into a module-level _parse_board_html(board_html) -> ParsedBoard (NamedTuple); search_boards now calls it and binds locals from the result. Added ParseBoardHtmlTests (4 fixture-based tests) covering a full board, a play-only board, download-id de-duplication, and empty HTML.
loop_result.evidence_change_is_honest: python3 -m py_compile passes; python3 -m unittest test_soundboard_snag runs 28 tests, all OK (24 prior + 4 new parse tests); --help exits 0; the extraction reuses the identical regexes/dedup/unescape logic so it is behavior-preserving; grep confirms the parse regexes (item-desc, btn-download-track, Category/Views/Tags) no longer appear in the search_boards body (only the render-label print remains), and search_boards shrank from ~734 to ~694 lines.

implementation_review.reason: F-002 is genuinely fixed: _parse_board_html and ParsedBoard exist, all parse regexes are gone from search_boards, all locals are correctly rebound at the call site, the counter/status/preview_count computations survive, and the four ParseBoardHtmlTests assert real field values at the new Interface.

builder_notes.pattern: Separate the pure decision from its side effect when extracting
builder_notes.how_to_recognize: A block computes a boolean/result AND mutates a counter or accumulator in the same branches.
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: Return the decision (and structured failure info) from a pure function; keep the mutation at the call site, driven by the returned info.
builder_notes.stack_example: Python: _evaluate_filters returns (meets, [(bucket_key, reason), ...]); the loop increments skipped_buckets[bucket_key] itself.

builder_notes.pattern: Preserve a subtle ordering rule explicitly in the extracted function
builder_notes.how_to_recognize: The original had an implicit dependency (e.g., 'only check the date filter if the basic filters passed').
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: Encode the dependency directly (here: evaluate the date branch only when there are no prior failures) and write a test that pins it.
builder_notes.stack_example: Python: a test asserts that when views fails, the date filter is not even evaluated.

builder_notes.pattern: Chip a large finding across loops with shrinking, named evidence
builder_notes.how_to_recognize: One finding is too big for a single honest loop (filter + date-scan + render).
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: Resolve one slice per loop, mark the finding carried_forward, and re-cite the residual evidence so the next loop's target is unambiguous.
builder_notes.stack_example: Python: F-006 evidence narrows from 'filter+render fused' to 'results render still inlined (1339-1395)'.

loops.narrative: Place — a good app, decomposing steadily and honestly. This loop extracts the filter decision into a pure, fully-branch-tested _evaluate_filters, separating it cleanly from the skipped_buckets side effect. F-006 is now half done; the remaining slice is the terminal rendering (which also absorbs the duplicated blocks of F-005). Ownership and concurrency stay trustworthy. The discipline to resist over-typing a 2-tuple and to keep the side effect at the call site is exactly the anti-overengineering the rubric rewards.

loop_result.what_changed: Extracted a pure _evaluate_filters(views_int, sound_count, approx_updated, min_views, min_sounds, recent_threshold, recent_days) from search_boards' inline filter block; it returns (meets, failures) where each failure is a (bucket_key, reason) tuple. search_boards now calls it, builds filter_reasons from the failures, and keeps the skipped_buckets attribution (gated on has_downloads) at the call site. Added EvaluateFiltersTests (6 tests) covering every branch including the date-only-when-basics-pass rule.
loop_result.evidence_change_is_honest: python3 -m py_compile passes; python3 -m unittest test_soundboard_snag runs 34 tests, all OK (28 prior + 6 new); --help exits 0; the extraction reproduces the original branch logic and side-effect attribution exactly (views and sounds can both fail and increment both buckets; the date branch is only evaluated when basics pass); grep confirms the inline filter logic (meets_filters = True / filter_reasons.append) no longer appears in search_boards; the function shrank to ~678 lines.

implementation_review.reason: The filter extraction is real, behavior-preserving, and introduces no regressions — all three checks pass.

findings.locality_impact: Drift between the two copies would produce inconsistent output for the same data.

findings.minimal_correction_path: Next loop: extract a pure _render_board_lines(board, stats, include_dates) -> list[str] for the final results render, fixture-tested; the search loop just prints the lines. Stop there — do not extract the progress/diagnostic prints (they are I/O-status, not data formatting). No renderer class.

simplification_check.structurally_necessary: Extracting the two format helpers passes the Deletion test (the duplicated blocks vanish into one definition each) and makes formatting fixture-testable. Pure functions returning strings; callers keep color/indent.
simplification_check.helpful_simplification: search_boards shrinks; output formatting is now testable and single-sourced.
simplification_check.should_not_be_done: Do not move ANSI color into the helpers (callers own presentation context/indent); do not extract progress/diagnostic prints; no renderer class hierarchy.
simplification_check.tests_after_fix: test_soundboard_snag.py gains FormatUpdatedLineTests (known/with-source/with-stats, unknown, unknown+stats) and FormatSkippedBreakdownTests (empty, fixed-order non-zero-only) at the new helper Interfaces.

backlog.title: Extract pure render/format helpers for the duplicated date + skipped-breakdown blocks (F-005)
backlog.why_it_matters: Removes drift-prone duplication and makes formatting testable; render-dedup slice of F-006.

backlog.title: Extract the per-board detail render into a pure tested helper (F-006 residual)
backlog.why_it_matters: Final render slice; shrinks search_boards to an orchestration core.

deepening_candidates.source_friction_proven: Finding F-006 — the detail render is sequential formatting inlined in the orchestration function
deepening_candidates.why_shallow_or_misplaced: There is no render Interface for the detail block; it is inlined.
deepening_candidates.behavior_to_move_behind_interface: BoardResult-to-lines formatting for the final results listing (name, URL, desc, category, views, dates via _format_updated_line, tags, sample files).
deepening_candidates.test_surface_after_change: test_soundboard_snag.py asserts the rendered lines for a representative BoardResult without network.
deepening_candidates.smallest_first_step: Extract _render_board_lines(board, stats, include_dates) -> list[str]; print the lines in the loop.
deepening_candidates.what_not_to_do: Do not extract progress/diagnostic prints; no renderer class hierarchy or templating layer.

builder_notes.pattern: Extract formatting as string-returning helpers, keep presentation context at the call site
builder_notes.how_to_recognize: Duplicated print blocks differ only in color or indent.
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: Return the semantic text from a pure helper; let each caller add its own color/indent wrapper.
builder_notes.stack_example: Python: _format_updated_line returns 'Updated: ...'; one caller prints it with a 2-space indent, the other without.

builder_notes.pattern: A fixed-order join helper beats repeated append blocks
builder_notes.how_to_recognize: Several `if count: parts.append(...)` lines repeated in two places.
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: Drive the parts from a (key, label) table in a pure function returning the joined string ('' when empty).
builder_notes.stack_example: Python: _format_skipped_breakdown iterates a labels table and joins non-zero buckets.

builder_notes.pattern: Know when a finding is nearly done and name the small residual honestly
builder_notes.how_to_recognize: After several slices, the remaining work is low-severity (non-duplicated sequential output).
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: Downgrade the residual's severity to match reality and consider whether the last slice is worth a loop or is an accepted residual.
builder_notes.stack_example: Python: F-006's remaining detail render is Noticeable, not Serious, once parse/filter/dedup are extracted.

loops.narrative: Place — a good app, nearly through its structural backlog. This loop resolves the duplicated render blocks with two pure, tested format helpers, lifting simplicity and test strategy and single-sourcing the formatting. F-005 is resolved; F-006 is down to one low-severity slice (the non-duplicated per-board detail render). Ownership and concurrency stay trustworthy. The refactor has stayed subtractive and test-backed throughout, resisting the renderer-class temptation the rubric warns against.

loop_result.what_changed: Extracted two pure formatting helpers from search_boards: _format_updated_line(approx_updated, approx_source, stats) -> str and _format_skipped_breakdown(skipped_buckets) -> str. Folded the four duplicated render sites (two date-display, two skipped-breakdown) into single calls, with each caller keeping its own color/indent. Added FormatUpdatedLineTests (3) and FormatSkippedBreakdownTests (2).
loop_result.evidence_change_is_honest: python3 -m py_compile passes; python3 -m unittest test_soundboard_snag runs 39 tests, all OK (34 prior + 5 new); --help exits 0; grep confirms breakdown_parts no longer appears anywhere and the date-display literals exist only inside the single _format_updated_line definition; the helpers reproduce the exact strings (including the 'approx', ' via {source}', and '; track headers: ok/total' fragments) and the date block keeps its 2-space indent at the inline site; search_boards shrank ~678->636 lines.

implementation_review.reason: All three checks pass: helpers exist at module level, all four duplicated blocks replaced, behavior is byte-identical, callers keep their own color and indent, tests assert real strings, and no regression introduced.

loops._spawn_isolation_note: Loop body inline in main (Opus); Step-3 reviewer + HALT challenger spawned as independent subagents.
loops.verdict: Good app, but not top-tier yet
loops.verdict_explanation: All the pure logic in search_boards is now extracted and tested: parse, filter, formatting, and (this loop) the per-board render. search_boards is down to its orchestration role. The remaining ceiling is that the orchestration itself (pagination, cross-page dedup, early-stop, near-miss suggestions) and the SoundboardSnag download pipeline are network-coupled and untested because there is no injectable fetch seam.

architecture_quality.proof: pure parse/filter/format extracted; the per-board render extraction is this loop's fix (scored next loop). Residual: no fetch seam, so the orchestration loop and SoundboardSnag pipeline are untestable

state_management.proof: single-threaded; one writer per concern; immutable instance attrs

domain_modeling.proof: BoardResult + ParsedBoard; sounds_info deliberately a plain (id,title) 2-tuple (guardrail: don't force types)

data_flow.proof: named-field contracts; residual: main re-filters results (soundboard-snag.py:1593) although search_boards already returns only downloadable boards (1299) — a redundant filter (F-007)

framework_idioms.proof: idiomatic stdlib; defensive sanitization; HTTPS; no secrets

concurrency.proof: no async/threading — synchronous CLI, no shared-mutable hazard
concurrency.residual_blocking_10: time.sleep pacing is intentional server etiquette, not a determinism hazard
concurrency.residual_rationale_or_backlog_ref: Tool is deliberately synchronous; no concurrency model to make safer. Permanent design carve-out.

simplicity.proof: render duplication removed via two pure helpers (commit 41ec0c7); search_boards shrank to ~636. Residual at loop start: the per-board detail render still inlined (addressed this loop)

test_strategy.proof: FormatUpdatedLineTests + FormatSkippedBreakdownTests added (commit 41ec0c7); 39 tests cover parse, filter and formatting at real Interfaces. Residual: network orchestration + download pipeline untested (F-008)

credibility.proof: parse, filter and formatting all test-backed; consistent behavior-preserving extraction history; code remains honest

strengths: Five behavior-preserving, independently-reviewed extractions (BoardResult, _parse_board_html, _evaluate_filters, two format helpers, render helper) — every slice tested at its Interface
search_boards is now an orchestration function; all of its pure logic is reachable and tested without the network
Synchronous design + defensive sanitization remain real strengths

findings.title: search_boards still inlines the per-board detail render
findings.why_it_matters: The detail render is sequential formatting that should be a tested helper, keeping search_boards larger than its orchestration role and the output untested.
findings.what_is_wrong: The final results listing inlined the per-board detail render (name/status, URL, description, category, views, dates, tags, sample files).
findings.leverage_impact: Maintainers read the detail render inside the orchestration function; output is untested.
findings.locality_impact: A formatting change is localized but was untested.
findings.why_weakens_submission: The last inlined render kept architecture and simplicity a notch below top-tier and the output untested.
findings.minimal_correction_path: Extract a pure _render_board_lines(board, stats, include_dates) -> list[str]; the loop prints the lines; fixture-test the lines' semantic content. No renderer class.

findings.title: Network orchestration and the download pipeline are untestable (no injectable fetch seam)
findings.why_it_matters: The genuinely complex untested logic — pagination, cross-page dedup, the early-stop on enough-downloadable, near-miss suggestions, and SoundboardSnag.snag's consecutive-failure abort — can only be exercised against the live site, so it has no regression guard.
findings.what_is_wrong: search_boards and SoundboardSnag call urlopen directly; there is no seam to inject a fake page-fetcher, so the orchestration and download loops cannot be unit-tested.
evidence: soundboard-snag.py:789-1404 (search_boards orchestration: pagination, dedup, early-stop, near-miss)
findings.leverage_impact: Callers/maintainers cannot verify the orchestration without the network.
findings.locality_impact: A regression in pagination or the early-stop would only surface against the live site.
findings.why_weakens_submission: The remaining test-strategy and architecture ceiling: the most behavior-rich code is untested. A fetch seam (real urlopen adapter + in-memory fake) satisfies the Unified Seam Policy two-adapter rule and unlocks deterministic tests.
findings.minimal_correction_path: Introduce a small fetch seam: a module-level _http_get(url) -> str default, threaded as an optional `fetch` parameter into search_boards (and a `fetcher` into SoundboardSnag). Production passes the default; tests pass an in-memory fake returning canned HTML keyed by URL. Then test pagination/dedup/early-stop/near-miss and the consecutive-failure abort. Two real adapters (urlopen + fake) justify the seam.

findings.title: main re-filters search results that are already downloadable-only
findings.why_it_matters: A redundant filter implies search_boards might return play-only boards, which it does not; the duplication is mildly misleading.
findings.what_is_wrong: main computes downloadable_boards = [r for r in results if r.has_downloads], but search_boards already returns only has_downloads boards.
findings.leverage_impact: None — the filter is a no-op.
findings.locality_impact: Minor: a reader may wonder whether search_boards can return play-only boards.
findings.why_weakens_submission: Redundant defensive code is a small data-flow/simplicity deduction.
findings.minimal_correction_path: Replace the re-filter with downloadable_boards = results (or iterate results directly), with a comment that search_boards already guarantees has_downloads.

simplification_check.structurally_necessary: Extracting _render_board_lines passes the Shallow-module test: a small Interface (board + stats + flag in, list[str] out) with the detail-render behavior behind it, now reached directly by tests. The render is pure (depends only on the BoardResult and module constants).
simplification_check.helpful_simplification: search_boards drops to ~615 lines and the results listing is now testable.
simplification_check.should_not_be_done: Do not move ANSI out of the render helper (the colors are intrinsic to each line); do not extract the progress/diagnostic prints; no renderer class. The F-008 fetch seam is a separate, larger change for a later loop.
simplification_check.tests_after_fix: test_soundboard_snag.py gains RenderBoardLinesTests (header/status, play-only, optional fields, dates-only-when-flag, sample files, empty samples) asserting semantic content with ANSI stripped.

backlog.title: Extract the per-board detail render into a pure tested _render_board_lines (F-006)
backlog.why_it_matters: Final render slice; makes the listing testable and shrinks search_boards to orchestration.

backlog.title: Introduce an injectable fetch seam to test the orchestration and download pipeline (F-008)
backlog.why_it_matters: The remaining test-strategy/architecture ceiling; unlocks deterministic tests of pagination, dedup, early-stop, near-miss, and the failure-abort.

backlog.title: Remove the redundant downloadable re-filter in main (F-007)
backlog.why_it_matters: Subtractive; clarifies that search_boards already guarantees downloadable-only.

deepening_candidates.candidate_module: Page-fetch seam for search_boards and SoundboardSnag
deepening_candidates.source_friction_proven: Finding F-008 — the orchestration and download loops cannot be tested without the network
deepening_candidates.why_shallow_or_misplaced: urlopen is called inline at multiple sites; there is no injection point for a deterministic fake.
deepening_candidates.behavior_to_move_behind_interface: HTTP GET of a page/track URL returning decoded text (or bytes for downloads), behind a default _http_get and an injectable fetch parameter.
deepening_candidates.test_surface_after_change: test_soundboard_snag.py drives search_boards with an in-memory fetcher returning canned HTML; asserts pagination, dedup, early-stop, near-miss, and the consecutive-failure abort.
deepening_candidates.smallest_first_step: Add _http_get(url) -> str and an optional fetch=_http_get parameter on search_boards; route the search-page and board-page reads through it.
deepening_candidates.what_not_to_do: Do not build an HTTP client class hierarchy or a registry of adapters; a single injected function is the honest seam.

builder_notes.pattern: Render functions return lines; the caller prints
builder_notes.how_to_recognize: A block of print() calls builds one logical unit of output inside a bigger loop.
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: Return a list[str] of formatted lines from a pure function; the loop prints them. Test the lines, not stdout.
builder_notes.stack_example: Python: _render_board_lines(board, stats, include_dates) -> list[str]; tests strip ANSI and assert semantic content.

builder_notes.pattern: Color is intrinsic to a render helper; do not factor it out
builder_notes.how_to_recognize: Each line interleaves several colored segments, unlike the earlier single-field format helpers.
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: Let the render helper own the color; assert on ANSI-stripped output in tests instead of forcing plain-text returns.
builder_notes.stack_example: Python: the board line mixes BOLD/CYAN/GREEN/GRAY — keep them in the helper.

builder_notes.pattern: Name the seam that unlocks the last untested surface
builder_notes.how_to_recognize: All pure logic is tested, but the network orchestration still has no regression guard.
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: Introduce a single injectable fetch function (default real, fake in tests) — two adapters justify the seam under the two-adapter rule.
builder_notes.stack_example: Python: search_boards(fetch=_http_get) lets tests pass an in-memory fetcher.

loops.narrative: Place — a good app, now with all of search_boards' pure logic extracted and tested. This loop extracts the per-board render into a fixture-tested helper, finishing the decomposition that began with BoardResult. The honest remaining ceiling is named precisely (F-008): the network orchestration and download pipeline have no injectable fetch seam, so the most behavior-rich code is untested. A single injected fetch function (real + fake) is the justified next move. The redundant re-filter in main (F-007) is a small subtractive cleanup. The refactor has stayed disciplined and test-backed throughout.

loop_result.what_changed: Extracted the per-board detail render out of search_boards into a pure _render_board_lines(board, stats, include_dates) -> list[str]; the results loop now prints the returned lines. Added RenderBoardLinesTests (6 tests) asserting header/status, play-only, optional-field presence, dates-only-when-flag, sample-file listing, and empty-samples, with ANSI stripped.
loop_result.evidence_change_is_honest: python3 -m py_compile passes; python3 -m unittest test_soundboard_snag runs 45 tests, all OK (39 prior + 6 new); --help exits 0; a direct render comparison shows the produced lines are byte-identical to the original prints (colors, the '\n'-prefixed Sample-files header, and the {idx:2} numbering preserved); search_boards shrank ~636->615 lines.

implementation_review.reason: The per-board render is fully extracted into a pure module-level _render_board_lines returning list[str]; the loop body is the required one-liner; all format strings, field order, and ANSI codes are byte-identical to the originals; six semantic tests cover the new interface with ANSI stripped; no regression introduced.
